stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Volcanoes form on the seafloor when magma erupts through cracks in the ocean crust.
  • A volcano in a phase of rapid eruptive activity is called an active submarine volcano.
  • Dormancy is a process in which a submarine volcano stops erupting and cools.
  • A submarine volcano that no longer erupts is called a dormant submarine volcano.
  • A dormant submarine volcano has entered the post-eruptive phase.
stem: The student wants to explain what a dormant submarine volcano is. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Before becoming dormant, an active submarine volcano will erupt at a rapid rate.
When it becomes dormant, an active submarine volcano enters the post-eruptive phase.
An active submarine volcano will stop erupting and eventually become dormant.
Having entered the post-eruptive phase, a dormant submarine volcano is one that no longer erupts.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer because it explains what a dormant submarine volcano is by pairing the term with its defining characteristic (no longer erupts) and its phase classification.

Choices A, B, and C describe related processes but don't define the dormant state itself.
